mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 250 in the CPIAUCSL index triggers central bank tightening, leading to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal, or Rank IC > 0.05 in inflationary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and payrolls (PAYEMS) signals robust economic activity, driving corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect between INDPRO and PAYEMS fails to produce positive returns in > 40% of cases, or joint coefficient significance drops below p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in GDP growth (GDPC1) below 2% annualized indicates weakening demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or GDPC1's predictive power loses significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market (UNRATE < 3.5%) increases wage pressures and corporate costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of cases when UNRATE < 3.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 in tight labor regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) above 1300 thousand units signal increased consumer confidence and demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of cases when HOUST > 1287, or HOUST's predictive power fails (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized decline in consumer spending (RSAFS) and GDP (GDPC1) indicates weakening demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction effect between RSAFS and GDPC1 fails to produce negative returns in > 45% of cases, or joint coefficient significance drops below p < 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 150k signals strong labor market performance, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 55% of cases when PAYEMS > 148662, or Rank IC < 0.03 in strong labor regimes.
